back_translation: Then Job answered:
'Truly I know that it is so, but how can a mortal be righteous before God?
If one wished to contend with Him, he could not answer Him once in a thousand times.
He is profoundly wise and mighty in strength. Who has ever resisted Him and prospered?
He moves mountains without their knowing, and overturns them in His anger.
He shakes the earth from its place, so that its pillars tremble.
He commands the sun not to shine, and seals up the stars.
He alone stretches out the heavens and treads on the waves of the sea.
He made the Bear, Orion, the Pleiades, and the constellations of the south.
He does great things beyond searching out, and wonders without number.
Behold, if He passes by me, I do not see Him; if He moves on, I do not perceive Him.
Behold, if He snatches away, who can stop Him? Who dares say to Him, 'What are You doing?'
God does not restrain His anger; even the helpers of Rahab cower beneath Him.
How then can I answer Him, or choose my words to argue with Him?
Even if I were righteous, I could not answer; I could only plead for mercy from my Judge.
Even if I called and He answered me, I would not believe that He was listening to my voice.
For He would crush me with a storm and multiply my wounds without cause.
He does not let me catch my breath, but fills me with bitterness.
If it is a matter of strength, behold, He is mighty! If of justice, who can summon Him to court?
Even if I were righteous, my own mouth would condemn me; even if I were blameless, it would prove me guilty.
I am blameless, yet I no longer care for myself; I despise my life.
It is all the same; therefore I say, 'He destroys both the blameless and the wicked.'
When a scourge brings sudden death, He mocks the despair of the innocent.
The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; He covers the faces of its judges. If it is not He, then who is it?
My days are swifter than a runner; they flee away without seeing any good.
They sweep by like boats of papyrus, like an eagle swooping down on its prey.
If I say, 'I will forget my complaint, change my expression, and smile,'
I still dread all my sufferings, for I know You will not hold me innocent.
Since I am already condemned, why should I labor in vain?
Even if I washed myself with snow and cleansed my hands with lye,
You would still plunge me into a filthy pit, so that even my own clothes would loathe me.
For He is not a man like me, that I could answer Him, that we could go to court together.
There is no mediator between us, to lay his hand on us both.
Let Him take His rod away from me, and let His terror not frighten me.
Then I would speak without fearing Him; but as it is, I am not so within myself.'
